tokenWindowPolicy.guidingPrinciple: Every session must reach a clean compile-and-test-passing state before stopping. No session should leave partially written files or failing tests. If a task looks too large, it must be split before starting.
sizingRules: S (Small)  — 1 new file, < 80 lines, no DB interaction. Fits alongside 2-3 other S tasks in one session.
M (Medium) — 1-2 files, 80-200 lines, or touches existing tested code. One or two M tasks per session.
L (Large)  — 200+ lines, multiple files, refactor of existing code, or integration test. One L task per session only.
XL         — Must be pre-split into L+M before starting. Never attempt an XL in one session.
hardRules: Always read CLAUDE.md, developer-notes.md, and the relevant domain contract section before writing any code.
Run mvn -DskipTests compile after every file is written. Fix compile errors before moving to the next file.
Run mvn test before marking a task complete. All tests must pass.
Write the CLAUDE.md session handoff note at the end of every session before stopping.
If you reach 80% of estimated token budget, stop at the next clean compile boundary, write the handoff note, and do not start the next task.

sessionGroups.name: Phase 2 — DecisionPolicy (part 2: rules 6-10) + stub RiskGuardService
sessionGroups.notes: DecisionPolicy rules 6-10 added in strict priority order after rules 1-5. Rule 6 (ENTER): fires only when no active session + qualified candidates present + guard strictly ALLOW — WARN excluded to be conservative. Rule 7 (BOOK_PROFIT): fires when session active + maxThetaProgressRatio >= 0.75 + livePnl strictly > 0.0 + guard ALLOW or WARN. Rule 8 (REDUCE): fires when session active + any signal has definite negative deltaAdjustedTheta2m; null values safely skipped. Rule 9 (HOLD/ALL_SIGNALS_HOLD): fires when session active + all signals HOLD thetaState + guard strictly ALLOW. Rule 10 (DEFAULT_HOLD): catches all remaining cases. Three new private helpers: maxThetaProgressRatio(), isDeltaAdjustedThetaNegative(), allSignalsHold(). RiskGuardService stub always returns ALLOW; Javadoc states 'Stub implementation. Full enforcement in Phase 4.' RiskGuardInput created with all 13 fields per Task 4.2 spec (pre-created in Phase 2 so evaluate() signature compiles). DecisionPolicyTest: 27 total @Test methods — 11 for rules 1-5 (unchanged from S2B) + 16 new for rules 6-10: rule6 (4), rule7 (4), rule8 (2), rule9 (2), rule10 (1), cross-rule invariants enterNeverEmitted_whenBlockingGuardActive + bookProfitNeverEmitted_whenPnlNotPositive + allRulePaths_rules6to10_returnCompleteCommand (3). Six new signal fixture helpers: signalWithRatio, signalWithNegativeDeltaAdjustedTheta, signalWithNullDeltaAdjustedTheta, signalInHoldState, signalWithThetaState, contextWithSignals. NOTE: mvn must be run on Windows host (Java 17 + Maven). Use build-and-test-s2c.bat.

sessionGroups.name: Phase 2 — Replay test across 3 market-day scenarios
sessionGroups.notes: Phase2ReplayTest exercises all three key decision paths through DecisionAgent in PAPER mode. sessionGroups.name: Phase 3 — PositionPlan records + PositionBuilderAgent (part 1: structure selection)
sessionGroups.notes: PositionPlanLeg is an immutable record with 8 fields: instrumentId, optionType, strike, expiryDate, lotSize, lots, entryPrice, side (enum Side {SHORT}). Compact constructor enforces non-null on all fields, lotSize>=1, lots>=1, entryPrice non-negative. PositionPlan is an immutable record with 10 fields: planId (UUID), plannedTs, underlying, legs (never null — List.copyOf), estimatedNetDelta, estimatedTotalPremium, structureType, lotCountPerLeg, riskGuardApproved, rejectionReason (Optional<String>). Compact constructor enforces: accepted plan must have at least one leg, rejected plan must have a rejectionReason. Static factory PositionPlan.rejected(underlying, structureType, reason) simplifies rejection return paths. PositionBuilderAgent uses a LotSizeLoader functional interface (same seam pattern as DecisionAgent) to load lot sizes from instrument_master — never hard-coded. buildShortStraddle() implements SHORT_STRADDLE only: groups qualified candidates by strike, finds pairs with both CE and PE, ranks by combined totalScore descending, checks |net delta| <= threshold using empiricalDelta2m (falling back to 5m, SOD, then 0.0). If all strikes are rejected by delta, returns NET_DELTA_EXCEEDS_THRESHOLD. Other rejection codes: NO_QUALIFIED_CANDIDATES, MISSING_CE_CANDIDATE, MISSING_PE_CANDIDATE, NO_MATCHING_STRIKE_PAIR, LOT_CAP_EXCEEDED, LOT_SIZE_LOOKUP_FAILED. INITIAL_LOTS_PER_LEG=1 constant; DEFAULT_MAX_NET_DELTA_THRESHOLD=0.15; DEFAULT_MAX_LOT_CAP=4. PositionBuilderAgentTest has 10 @Test methods covering: valid straddle (correct 2 legs, lot size from stub loader, entry price from candidate, net delta computed, total premium computed), planId non-null, delta rejection, fallback to second strike when first exceeds threshold, no candidates (empty list), all disqualified, missing CE, missing PE, best strike selected by score, no-signal fallback to zero delta. Stub LotSizeLoader: NIFTY=65, BANKNIFTY=30. All tests are pure in-memory — no DB, no HTTP. NOTE: mvn must be run on Windows host (Java 17 + Maven required). Use build-and-test-s3a.bat.

sessionGroups.name: Phase 3 — PositionBuilderAgent (part 2: strangle) + wire into DecisionAgent
sessionGroups.notes: Added STRUCTURE_SHORT_STRANGLE='SHORT_STRANGLE' constant and buildShortStrangle() to PositionBuilderAgent. Strangle picks best-ranked CE and best-ranked PE independently by totalScore; CE and PE must be at different strikes. Same delta threshold (|net delta|<=maxNetDeltaThreshold) and same rejection code pattern as straddle. New rejection code NO_MATCHING_STRANGLE_PAIR when all CE/PE pairs share the same strike. Added 3 strangle tests to PositionBuilderAgentTest. Wired PositionBuilderAgent into DecisionAgent via two new functional interface seams: PlanBuilder (builds PositionPlan from candidates/signals) and SessionPersister (persists new PositionSessionSnapshot). DecisionAgent constructor gained 2 new params (underlying + planBuilder + sessionPersister) with a 10-param full constructor and a convenience 10-param Phase2 constructor that passes null for the builder seams. Phase2ReplayTest.buildAgentWithLivePnl() updated to pass underlying='NIFTY'. ENTER path in decide(): if PlanBuilder is non-null → build plan → if rejected → rebind to SKIP with rejection reason → if accepted → buildSessionFromPlan() → persist → rebind positionSessionId on command. Created Phase3EnterSessionTest with 2 tests: (1) simulated ENTER creates session with exactly 2 legs (1 CE + 1 PE), command carries session ID; (2) disqualified candidates → SKIP, no session created. All 254 tests pass. NOTE: DecisionAgent.buildSessionFromPlan() defaults DTE=0 and spot=0 — real values wired in Phase 4/5 when live market data is available.

sessionGroups.name: Phase 4 — RiskGuardService (part 2: 4 remaining stops) + command gating
sessionGroups.notes: Completed the remaining four hard stops in RiskGuardService: total drawdown / max loss breach → FORCE_EXIT, churn detection → HALT_SESSION, lot cap breach → BLOCK_NEW_ENTRY, and missing required signal data on open legs → BLOCK_NEW_ENTRY. RiskGuardService now aggregates all active violations, escalates by highest RiskGuardDecision severity, and preserves every triggered condition in the returned snapshot/explanation. DecisionAgent now applies Risk Guard after policy evaluation and before any command side effects, so ENTER/ADD can be downgraded to SKIP, HOLD can be forced to EXIT_ALL, and FORCE_REDUCE is emitted before any session mutation path runs. tasks.subject: Build CandidateScoringEngine — premium richness + liquidity + disqualification
acceptanceCriteria: Premium richness score (points + pct) computed vs options_context_buckets cohort

tasks.notes: CandidateScoringEngine.java: pure scoring class, no DB access. CohortKey is a package-private record inside the engine class keyed by (underlying, optionType, moneynessBucket, timeBucket15m). Premium richness: points = lastPrice - historicalAvgPrice; pct = points/avg * 100; score linearly interpolates 0%-40% to 0.0-1.0 (RICHNESS_SATURATION_PCT=40). Liquidity: 50/50 weighted average of spread score (1 - spreadRatio/MAX_SPREAD_RATIO, clamped) and volume score (volume / (2x avgVolume), clamped). All 4 disqualifiers implemented in priority order: DTE_OUT_OF_WINDOW checked first (MIN_DTE_DAYS=1, MAX_DTE_DAYS=7), then ZERO_BID, ZERO_VOLUME, MISSING_COHORT. Theta/delta scores are stubbed 0.0 — added in S1C. All 4 weight constants defined (WEIGHT_THETA=0.40, WEIGHT_RICHNESS=0.30, WEIGHT_LIQUIDITY=0.20, WEIGHT_DELTA_RISK=0.10, sum=1.0). totalScore is the full weighted sum (theta+delta contribute 0.0 in S1B). Test covers 9 scenarios including: normal scoring, all 4 disqualifiers, richness math (known inputs, below-avg, above-saturation), liquidity edge cases (tight+high, wide+low), and weight sum sanity. Time-proximity linearly interpolates timeBucket15m from THETA_LOW_DECAY_TIME_BUCKET(26)→0.0 to THETA_HIGH_DECAY_TIME_BUCKET(8)→1.0. deltaRiskScore: uses |moneynessPoints|; contracts at absMoneyness<=DELTA_RISK_NEAR_ATM_THRESHOLD_POINTS(100) receive a score capped at DELTA_RISK_NEAR_ATM_PENALTY_CAP(0.30); contracts between 100 and DELTA_RISK_SAFE_DISTANCE_POINTS(500) are linearly interpolated from 0.30→1.0; beyond 500 the score saturates at 1.0. Session S1C delivered Tasks 1.3b and 1.4. CandidateScoringEngine.java now implements all four scoring components — the two stub methods from S1B are fully replaced. thetaOpportunityScore is a weighted composite (THETA_WEIGHT_RICHNESS=0.60 × richnessScore + THETA_WEIGHT_TIME_PROXIMITY=0.40 × timeProximityScore). Time proximity is linearly interpolated: timeBucket15m ≤ THETA_HIGH_DECAY_TIME_BUCKET(8) → 1.0, ≥ THETA_LOW_DECAY_TIME_BUCKET(26) → 0.0. deltaRiskScore is based on |moneynessPoints|: the near-ATM zone (0–DELTA_RISK_NEAR_ATM_THRESHOLD_POINTS=100 pts) scores linearly from 0.0 to DELTA_RISK_NEAR_ATM_PENALTY_CAP(0.30); the OTM zone (100–DELTA_RISK_SAFE_DISTANCE_POINTS=500 pts) linearly interpolates from the penalty cap to 1.0; beyond 500 pts the score saturates at 1.0. Session S1B delivered Task 1.3a. CandidateScoringEngine.java is a pure scoring class with zero DB access — all input data arrives via method parameters. The engine's public API is a single score(RawContractRow, Map<CohortKey,OptionsContextBucket>, scanTs, today) method that returns a fully-populated CandidateOpportunity. CohortKey is a package-private record nested inside the engine (same package as the test) keyed by (underlying, optionType, moneynessBucket, timeBucket15m). Premium richness: richnessPoints = lastPrice − historicalAvgPrice (points); richnessPct = points/avg × 100; richnessScore linearly scales 0%→0.0 to RICHNESS_SATURATION_PCT(40%)→1.0 and caps there — negative richness yields 0.0, not a penalty. Liquidity: 50/50 blend of spreadScore (1 − spreadRatio/MAX_SPREAD_RATIO, clamped 0-1, where MAX_SPREAD_RATIO=0.05) and volumeScore (volume / (VOLUME_SATURATION_MULTIPLE × avgVolume), clamped 0-1, where VOLUME_SATURATION_MULTIPLE=2.0). All four weight constants defined as public static final doubles: WEIGHT_THETA=0.40, WEIGHT_RICHNESS=0.30, WEIGHT_LIQUIDITY=0.20, WEIGHT_DELTA_RISK=0.10 (sum=1.0 verified by test). Theta and delta scores are stubbed as 0.0 — they contribute to the totalScore formula but are zero until S1C. Disqualification order: DTE_OUT_OF_WINDOW (checked first, MIN_DTE_DAYS=1, MAX_DTE_DAYS=7), ZERO_BID, ZERO_VOLUME, MISSING_COHORT. Disqualified contracts carry all raw contract fields but zero scores and Optional.of(reasonCode).
